summary.intent: Implement the framework-agnostic DatePicker state machine (issue #289, epic #224) owning popover open/close, date selection, input parse/validation, and ARIA/data attributes. Mid-session, the user also requested fixing locale date-order handling, which expanded into exposing a private CLDR-backed function in ars-i18n.
summary.outcome: Delivered DatePicker core with 107 tests (100% function / 99.84% line coverage), fixed 9 spec drifts, exposed ars-i18n::date_order() for CLDR-accurate locale ordering, rewired DateField to use it, fixed a snapshot-count gate bug that silently skipped two entire component categories (date-time, data-display), and passed cargo xci-fast. Session ended mid-commit before the PR was opened.

repo: The Machine trait requires `type Effect: EffectName` — effects must be typed enums, not strings. Spec §1.7 used string effect names + use_platform_effects() closures in core, which is both non-compilable and violates the convention.

Focus effects are adapter-driven: core emits PendingEffect::named(Effect::FocusCalendar) etc.; the adapter resolves actual DOM focus. This matches Popover/Dialog convention and the issue's element/ref note.
CalendarDate has no public fields — use accessors year() -> i32, month() -> u8, day() -> u8. nzu8 and NonZero do not exist anywhere in the codebase. new_gregorian(i32, u8, u8) -> Result<...>.
CalendarDate has no PartialOrd/Ord — ordering requires .compare(other) -> Ordering (not >=/<= operators).
ComponentIds has no public base_id field — use ids.id() accessor.
IsDateUnavailableFn (= Callback<dyn Fn(&CalendarDate)->bool + Send+Sync>) is the calendar type for the unavailability predicate, not fn(&CalendarDate) -> bool.
Service::new takes props by value + &Env + &Messages by reference. send is #[must_use] — requires drop(svc.send(...)).

Context only derives Clone; manual Debug is needed when the struct contains MessageFn fields (which aren't Debug). Don't derive PartialEq on Context when Messages is included. Mirrors Calendar's pattern.
Every sibling component (Calendar, DateField, Popover) has on_props_changed + initial_effects + a SyncProps(Box<Props>) event for controlled-prop sync. This was entirely absent from the spec.
Bindable::sync_controlled + sync_props_into_ctx is the canonical controlled/uncontrolled re-derivation pattern.

ars-i18n already had a complete private date_order(locale) -> DateOrder (CLDR via ICU4X probe, JS Intl.DateTimeFormat().formatToParts on wasm, (language, region) heuristic fallback) — it was just private. DateField and DatePicker each had their own partial reimplementations of only the heuristic fallback.

is_component_category in xtask/src/lint.rs matched hyphenated category names ("date-time", "data-display") while source dirs and snapshot file segments use underscores — causing the snapshot-count gate to silently skip two entire component categories.
ConnectApi trait requires only `type Part` and `fn part_attrs` (2-item trait, not more).
rating_group was already under the 9-snapshot floor (had 8) — invisible until the gate bug was fixed.
ars-components depends on ars-i18n with default-features = false; the i18n feature (ars-i18n/icu4x) enables the CLDR path. Default builds use the heuristic fallback.

code.finding: Private date_order(locale) function implementing CLDR/ICU4X path, JS Intl path (wasm), and (language, region) heuristic fallback. Was private; exposed in this session.
Existing tests for date_order covering en-US→MDY, en-CA→YMD, de-DE→DMY, ja-JP→YMD — contract was already locked before this session.
segment_order_for_locale was a hand-rolled (language, region) heuristic with a correct DMY default. Rewired to call ars-i18n::date_order() in this session.
Event::SyncProps(_) => None exhaustiveness arm — genuinely unreachable (SyncProps is handled before the match). The 1 uncovered line in final 99.84% line coverage.

is_component_category bug: matched hyphen-separated category names but component dirs and snapshot segments use underscores, causing silent skip of date-time and data-display categories in the snapshot-count gate.

workflow: Write tests first (TDD): run to confirm red, then implement. Proptest found two real bugs before they shipped: (a) redundant open Bindable diverged from State under controlled→uncontrolled flip; (b) SelectDate with close_on_select=false wasn't updating open Bindable's internal value.
When a proptest invariant fails, the shrunk counterexample is the debugging unit — read it carefully before jumping to fixes.
cargo insta accept does not take -p — accept is workspace-wide; use cargo insta test -p for scoped runs.
spec-compile-snippets xtask only does syn syntax parse, not type/borrow checking. Blocks can opt out with rust,no_check. Spec code can be illustrative rather than fully compilable.
Coverage analysis with llvm-cov reveals unreachable exhaustiveness arms vs. real uncovered branches — distinguish them before writing gap-filling tests.

Proptest invariants must account for framework behavior: set_props is a no-op when props are unchanged, so asserting that a controlled-open flip forces state to match the new prop is wrong — on_props_changed only fires when props actually differ.
cargo xci-fast is the mandatory pre-push gate; cargo xci (full) additionally validates wasm-coverage and feature-matrix groups.

friction: Spec §1.7 had ~10 compile-blockers vs. the real codebase: missing type Effect, string effect names, CalendarDate field/nzu8/NonZero that don't exist, >=/<= on a non-Ord type, ids.base_id nonexistent, fn instead of IsDateUnavailableFn, by-value Service::new, omitted on_props_changed/initial_effects. Extensive cross-referencing of real source was required before any code could be written.
Session hit the conversation limit mid-implementation and had to continue in a new context window.
Proptest found two sequential real bugs requiring multiple diagnosis+fix rounds before settling on State-as-source-of-truth design.
cargo insta accept -p ars-components syntax error discovered during usage — the -p flag is not supported by accept.

Snapshot-count gate silently skipped two entire component categories (date-time, data-display) due to hyphen vs. underscore mismatch — a pre-existing blind spot that required scope expansion (fixing rating_group) when the bug was corrected.
Stale staged change in pagination/mod.rs (from before the session) failed nightly-fmt xci-fast check, blocking the push flow.
Closure-lifetime errors in handler tests required explicit let bindings for service references.
Background bash task outputs were hard to retrieve — needed multiple follow-up commands to read results.
open_items: PR still needs to be opened — transcript ends mid-commit before the PR is created.
Pre-existing utility/highlight snapshot orphans still block bare cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject (feature-gated tests not run in default build).
Pre-existing 94 cargo doc warnings across the crate (none introduced by this change).
WebIntlBackend::date_order wasm path in ars-i18n is untested locally — only nightly wasm-coverage CI validates it.
Adapter implementations (Leptos/Dioxus) for DatePicker are out of scope — separate tasks.

date_order() exposes only field order, not locale-aware separators (. vs /). Separator is still a small heuristic in default_format_for_locale. Full CLDR separator derivation would require additional i18n API surface.
cargo xci (full, not fast) should be run before final merge to exercise wasm-coverage and feature-matrix groups.
